# Security Standards And Sources

Browse primary sources when the user asks for current standards, compliance-adjacent work, or "latest" security guidance.

## Primary Sources

- OWASP ASVS: https://owasp.org/www-project-application-security-verification-standard/
- OWASP ASVS GitHub: https://github.com/OWASP/ASVS
- OWASP Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-ten/
- OWASP Top 10 2025 introduction: https://owasp.org/Top10/2025/0x00_2025-Introduction/
- OWASP Cheat Sheet Series: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/
- OWASP Web Security Testing Guide: https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/
- OWASP API Security Top 10 2023: https://owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x00-header/
- OWASP GenAI Security Project / LLM Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications
- NIST SSDF SP 800-218: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final
- CISA Secure by Design: https://www.cisa.gov/securebydesign
- MDN Web Security: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security
- MDN HTTP headers: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers
- web.dev security headers: https://web.dev/articles/security-headers

## Use Guidance

- Use OWASP ASVS for requirement-level architecture and verification.
- Use OWASP Top 10 for prioritization and executive-level risk framing.
- Use OWASP Cheat Sheets for implementation details.
- Use WSTG for authorized testing plans.
- Use API Top 10 for API products and mobile/web backends.
- Use NIST SSDF/CISA Secure by Design for SDLC, governance, and product accountability.
- Use MDN/web.dev for browser headers and web-platform behavior.

## Source Discipline

- Prefer official docs over blog posts.
- Include version/date when citing standards.
- Do not treat automated scanner output as truth without code/path validation.
- Do not claim compliance from a quick audit; state scope and evidence.
